summary: DRAM companies are deeply undervalued relative to semi-cap equipment companies, and Baker knows of no one else who is bearish on DRAM — a sign of dangerous consensus on semi-cap

tickers_audited.reasoning: Micron is the primary US-listed DRAM producer and is the direct subject of the DRAM undervaluation thesis. Baker's relative value argument centers on DRAM companies being cheap vs. semi-cap equipment, and MU is the canonical representative of that trade.

Baker explicitly names Astera Labs as a high-conviction long he has held since Series C, making it a direct subject of a distinct sub-thesis discussed in the same segment. The summary explicitly calls this out as a named position.

While KLAC is a semi-cap equipment company that fits the general category Baker is bearish on, the transcript summary does not confirm Baker specifically named KLAC as the expensive side of the trade. The thesis references 'semi-cap equipment companies' generically. Without explicit mention of KLAC by name in connection with this thesis, it should be removed per the strict standard.
